6
7 type
8 ResultError*[E] = object of ValueError
9 ## Error raised when using `tryValue` value of result when error is set
10 ## See also Exception bridge mode
11 error*: E
12
13 ResultDefect* = object of Defect
14 ## Defect raised when accessing value when error is set and vice versa
15 ## See also Exception bridge mode
16
17 Result*[T, E] = object
18 ## Result type that can hold either a value or an error, but not both
19 ##
20 ## # Example
21 ##
22 ## ```
23 ## import results
24 ##
25 ## # Re-export `results` so that API is always available to users of your module!
26 ## export results
27 ##
28 ## # It's convenient to create an alias - most likely, you'll do just fine
29 ## # with strings or cstrings as error for a start
30 ##
31 ## type R = Result[int, string]
32 ##
33 ## # Once you have a type, use `ok` and `err`:
34 ##
35 ## func works(): R =
36 ## # ok says it went... ok!
37 ## R.ok 42
38 ## func fails(): R =
39 ## # or type it like this, to not repeat the type:
40 ## result.err "bad luck"
41 ##
42 ## func alsoWorks(): R =
43 ## # or just use the shortcut - auto-deduced from the return type!
44 ## ok(24)
45 ##
46 ## if (let w = works(); w.isOk):
47 ## echo w[], " or use value: ", w.value
48 ##
49 ## # In case you think your callers want to differentiate between errors:
50 ## type
51 ## Error = enum
52 ## a, b, c
53 ## type RE[T] = Result[T, Error]
54 ##
55 ## # You can use the question mark operator to pass errors up the call stack
56 ## func f(): R =
57 ## let x = ?works() - ?fails()
58 ## assert false, "will never reach"
59 ##
60 ## # If you provide this exception converter, this exception will be raised on
61 ## # `tryValue`:
62 ## func toException(v: Error): ref CatchableError = (ref CatchableError)(msg: $v)
63 ## try:
64 ## RE[int].err(a).tryValue()
65 ## except CatchableError:
66 ## echo "in here!"
67 ##
68 ## # You can use `Opt[T]` as a replacement for `Option` = `Opt` is an alias for
69 ## # `Result[T, void]`, meaning you can use the full `Result` API on it:
70 ## let x = Opt[int].ok(42)
71 ## echo x.get()
72 ##
73 ## # ... or `Result[void, E]` as a replacement for `bool`, providing extra error
74 ## # information!
75 ## let y = Result[void, string].err("computation failed")
76 ## echo y.error()
77 ##
78 ## ```
79 ##
80 ## See the tests for more practical examples, specially when working with
81 ## back and forth with the exception world!
82 ##
83 ## # Potential benefits:
84 ##
85 ## * Handling errors becomes explicit and mandatory at the call site -
86 ## goodbye "out of sight, out of mind"
87 ## * Errors are a visible part of the API - when they change, so must the
88 ## calling code and compiler will point this out - nice!
89 ## * Errors are a visible part of the API - your fellow programmer is
90 ## reminded that things actually can go wrong
91 ## * Jives well with Nim `discard`
92 ## * Jives well with the new Defect exception hierarchy, where defects
93 ## are raised for unrecoverable errors and the rest of the API uses
94 ## results
95 ## * Error and value return have similar performance characteristics
96 ## * Caller can choose to turn them into exceptions at low cost - flexible
97 ## for libraries!
98 ## * Mostly relies on simple Nim features - though this library is no
99 ## exception in that compiler bugs were discovered writing it :)
100 ##
101 ## # Potential costs:
102 ##
103 ## * Handling errors becomes explicit and mandatory - if you'd rather ignore
104 ## them or just pass them to some catch-all, this is noise
105 ## * When composing operations, value must be lifted before processing,
106 ## adding potential verbosity / noise (fancy macro, anyone?)
107 ## * There's no call stack captured by default (see also `catch` and
108 ## `capture`)
109 ## * The extra branching may be more expensive for the non-error path
110 ## (though this can be minimized with PGO)
111 ##
112 ## The API visibility issue of exceptions can also be solved with
113 ## `{.raises.}` annotations - as of now, the compiler doesn't remind
114 ## you to do so, even though it knows what the right annotation should be.
115 ## `{.raises.}` does not participate in generic typing, making it just as
116 ## verbose but less flexible in some ways, if you want to type it out.
117 ##
118 ## Many system languages make a distinction between errors you want to
119 ## handle and those that are simply bugs or unrealistic to deal with..
120 ## handling the latter will often involve aborting or crashing the funcess -
121 ## reliable systems like Erlang will try to relaunch it.
122 ##
123 ## On the flip side we have dynamic languages like python where there's
124 ## nothing exceptional about exceptions (hello StopIterator). Python is
125 ## rarely used to build reliable systems - its strengths lie elsewhere.
126 ##
127 ## # Exception bridge mode
128 ##
129 ## When the error of a `Result` is an `Exception`, or a `toException` helper
130 ## is present for your error type, the "Exception bridge mode" is
131 ## enabled and instead of raising `ResultError`, `tryValue` will raise the
132 ## given `Exception` on access. `[]` and `value` will continue to raise a
133 ## `Defect`.
134 ##
135 ## This is an experimental feature that may be removed.
136 ##
137 ## # Other languages
138 ##
139 ## Result-style error handling seems pretty popular lately, specially with
140 ## statically typed languages:
141 ## Haskell: https://hackage.haskell.org/package/base-4.11.1.0/docs/Data-Either.html
142 ## Rust: https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/result/enum.Result.html
143 ## Modern C++: https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/utility/expected
144 ## More C++: https://github.com/ned14/outcome
145 ##
146 ## Swift is interesting in that it uses a non-exception implementation but
147 ## calls errors exceptions and has lots of syntactic sugar to make them feel
148 ## that way by implicitly passing them up the call chain - with a mandatory
149 ## annotation that function may throw:
150 ## https://developer.apple.com/library/content/documentation/Swift/Conceptual/Swift_Programming_Language/ErrorHandling.html
151 ##
152 ## # Considerations for the error type
153 ##
154 ## * Use a `string` or a `cstring` if you want to provide a diagnostic for
155 ## the caller without an expectation that they will differentiate between
156 ## different errors. Callers should never parse the given string!
157 ## * Use an `enum` to provide in-depth errors where the caller is expected
158 ## to have different logic for different errors
159 ## * Use a complex type to include error-specific meta-data - or make the
160 ## meta-data collection a visible part of your API in another way - this
161 ## way it remains discoverable by the caller!
162 ##
163 ## A natural "error API" progression is starting with `Opt[T]`, then
164 ## `Result[T, cstring]`, `Result[T, enum]` and `Result[T, object]` in
165 ## escalating order of complexity.
166 ##
167 ## # Result equivalences with other types
168 ##
169 ## Result allows tightly controlling the amount of information that a
170 ## function gives to the caller:
171 ##
172 ## ## `Result[void, void] == bool`
173 ##
174 ## Neither value nor error information, it either worked or didn't. Most
175 ## often used for `proc`:s with side effects.
176 ##
177 ## ## `Result[T, void] == Option[T]`
178 ##
179 ## Return value if it worked, else tell the caller it failed. Most often
180 ## used for simple computations.
181 ##
182 ## Works as a replacement for `Option[T]` (aliased as `Opt[T]`)
183 ##
184 ## ## `Result[T, E]` -
185 ##
186 ## Return value if it worked, or a statically known piece of information
187 ## when it didn't - most often used when a function can fail in more than
188 ## one way - E is typically a `string` or an `enum`.
189 ##
190 ## ## `Result[T, ref E]`
191 ##
192 ## Returning a `ref E` allows introducing dynamically typed error
193 ## information, similar to exceptions.
194 ##
195 ## # Other implemenations in nim
196 ##
197 ## There are other implementations in nim that you might prefer:
198 ## * Either from nimfp: https://github.com/vegansk/nimfp/blob/master/src/fp/either.nim
199 ## * result_type: https://github.com/kapralos/result_type/
200 ##
201 ## `Option` compatibility
202 ##
203 ## `Result[T, void]` is similar to `Option[T]`, except it can be used with
204 ## all `Result` operators and helpers.
205 ##
206 ## One difference is `Option[ref|ptr T]` which disallows `nil` - `Opt[T]`
207 ## allows an "ok" result to hold `nil` - this can be useful when `nil` is
208 ## a valid outcome of a function, but increases complexity for the caller.
209 ##
210 ## # Implementation notes
211 ##
212 ## This implementation is mostly based on the one in rust. Compared to it,
213 ## there are a few differences - if know of creative ways to improve things,
214 ## I'm all ears.
215 ##
216 ## * Rust has the enum variants which lend themselves to nice construction
217 ## where the full Result type isn't needed: `Err("some error")` doesn't
218 ## need to know value type - maybe some creative converter or something
219 ## can deal with this?
220 ## * Nim templates allow us to fail fast without extra effort, meaning the
221 ## other side of `and`/`or` isn't evaluated unless necessary - nice!
222 ## * Rust uses From traits to deal with result translation as the result
223 ## travels up the call stack - needs more tinkering - some implicit
224 ## conversions would be nice here
225 ## * Pattern matching in rust allows convenient extraction of value or error
226 ## in one go.
227 ##
228 ## # Performance considerations
229 ##
230 ## When returning a Result instead of a simple value, there are a few things
231 ## to take into consideration - in general, we are returning more
232 ## information directly to the caller which has an associated cost.
233 ##
234 ## Result is a value type, thus its performance characteristics
235 ## generally follow the performance of copying the value or error that
236 ## it stores. `Result` would benefit greatly from "move" support in the
237 ## language.
238 ##
239 ## In many cases, these performance costs are negligeable, but nonetheless
240 ## they are important to be aware of, to structure your code in an efficient
241 ## manner:
242 ##
243 ## * Memory overhead
244 ## Result is stored in memory as a union with a `bool` discriminator -
245 ## alignment makes it somewhat tricky to give an exact size, but in
246 ## general, `Result[int, int]` will take up `2*sizeof(int)` bytes:
247 ## 1 `int` for the discriminator and padding, 1 `int` for either the value
248 ## or the error. The additional size means that returning may take up more
249 ## registers or spill onto the stack.
250 ## * Loss of RVO
251 ## Nim does return-value-optimization by rewriting `proc f(): X` into
252 ## `proc f(result: var X)` - in an expression like `let x = f()`, this
253 ## allows it to avoid a copy from the "temporary" return value to `x` -
254 ## when using Result, this copy currently happens always because you need
255 ## to fetch the value from the Result in a second step: `let x = f().value`
256 ## * Extra copies
257 ## To avoid spurious evaluation of expressions in templates, we use a
258 ## temporary variable sometimes - this means an unnecessary copy for some
259 ## types.
260 ## * Bad codegen
261 ## When doing RVO, Nim generates poor and slow code: it uses a construct
262 ## called `genericReset` that will zero-initialize a value using dynamic
263 ## RTTI - a process that the C compiler subsequently is unable to
264 ## optimize. This applies to all types, but is exacerbated with Result
265 ## because of its bigger footprint - this should be fixed in compiler.
266 ## * Double zero-initialization bug
267 ## Nim has an initialization bug that causes additional poor performance:
268 ## `var x = f()` will be expanded into `var x; zeroInit(x); f(x)` where
269 ## `f(x)` will call the slow `genericReset` and zero-init `x` again,
270 ## unnecessarily.
271 ##
272 ## Comparing `Result` performance to exceptions in Nim is difficult - it
273 ## will depend on the error type used, the frequency at which exceptions
274 ## happen, the amount of error handling code in the application and the
275 ## compiler and backend used.
276 ##
277 ## * the default C backend in nim uses `setjmp` for exception handling -
278 ## the relative performance of the happy path will depend on the structure
279 ## of the code: how many exception handlers there are, how much unwinding
280 ## happens. `setjmp` works by taking a snapshot of the full CPU state and
281 ## saving it to memory when enterting a try block (or an implict try
282 ## block, such as is introduced with `defer` and similar constructs).
283 ## * an efficient exception handling mechanism (like the C++ backend or
284 ## `nlvm`) will usually have a lower cost on the happy path because the
285 ## value can be returned more efficiently. However, there is still a code
286 ## and data size increase depending on the specific situation, as well as
287 ## loss of optimization opportunities to consider.
288 ## * raising an exception is usually (a lot) slower than returning an error
289 ## through a Result - at raise time, capturing a call stack and allocating
290 ## memory for the Exception is expensive, so the performance difference
291 ## comes down to the complexity of the error type used.
292 ## * checking for errors with Result is local branching operation that also
293 ## happens on the happy path - this may be a cost.
294 ##
295 ## An accurate summary might be that Exceptions are at its most efficient
296 ## when errors are not handled and don't happen.
297 ##
298 ## # Relevant nim bugs
299 ##
300 ## https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/13799 - type issues
301 ## https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/8745 - genericReset slow
302 ## https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/13879 - double-zero-init slow
303 ## https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/14318 - generic error raises pragma (fixed in 1.6.14+)
304 ## https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/23741 - inefficient codegen for temporaries
305 ## https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/20699 (fixed in 2.0.0+)
306 # case oResultPrivate: bool
307 # of false:
308 # eResultPrivate: E
309 # of true:
310 # vResultPrivate: T
311
312 # ResultPrivate* works around (fixed in 1.6.14+):
313 # * https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/3770
314 # * https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/20900
315 #
316 # Do not use these fields directly in your code, they're not meant to be
317 # public!
318 when T is void:
319 when E is void:
320 oResultPrivate*: bool
321 else:
322 case oResultPrivate*: bool
323 of false:
324 eResultPrivate*: E
325 of true:
326 discard
327 else:
328 when E is void:
329 case oResultPrivate*: bool
330 of false:
331 discard
332 of true:
333 vResultPrivate*: T
334 else:
335 case oResultPrivate*: bool
336 of false:
337 eResultPrivate*: E
338 of true:
339 vResultPrivate*: T
340
341 Opt*[T] = Result[T, void]
342
343 const
344 resultsGenericsOpenSym* {.booldefine.} = true
345 ## Enable the experimental `genericsOpenSym` feature or a workaround for the
346 ## template injection problem in the issue linked below where scoped symbol
347 ## resolution works differently for expanded bodies in templates depending on
348 ## whether we're in a generic context or not.
349 ##
350 ## The issue leads to surprising errors where symbols from outer scopes get
351 ## bound instead of the symbol created in the template scope which should be
352 ## seen as a better candidate, breaking access to `error` in `valueOr` and
353 ## friends.
354 ##
355 ## In Nim versions that do not support `genericsOpenSym`, a macro is used
356 ## instead to reassign symbol matches which may or may not work depending on
357 ## the complexity of the code.
358 ##
359 ## Nim 2.0.8 was released with an incomplete fix but already declares
360 ## `nimHasGenericsOpenSym`.
361 # TODO https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/22605
362 # TODO https://github.com/arnetheduck/nim-results/issues/34
363 # TODO https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/23386
364 # TODO https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/23385
365 #
366 # Related PR:s (there's more probably, but this gives an overview)
367 # https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/pull/23102
368 # https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/pull/23572
369 # https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/pull/23873
370 # https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/pull/23892
371 # https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/pull/23939
372
373 resultsGenericsOpenSymWorkaround* {.booldefine.} =
374 resultsGenericsOpenSym and not defined(nimHasGenericsOpenSym2)
375 ## Prefer macro workaround to solve genericsOpenSym issue
376 # TODO https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/pull/23892#discussion_r1713434311
377
378 resultsGenericsOpenSymWorkaroundHint* {.booldefine.} = true
379
380 resultsLent {.booldefine.} =
381 (NimMajor, NimMinor, NimPatch) >= (2, 2, 0) or
382 (defined(gcRefc) and ((NimMajor, NimMinor, NimPatch) >= (2, 0, 8)))
383 ## Enable return of `lent` types - this *mostly* works in Nim 1.6.18+ but
384 ## there have been edge cases reported as late as 1.6.14 - YMMV -
385 ## conservatively, `lent` is therefore enabled only with the latest Nim
386 ## version at the time of writing, where it could be verified to work with
387 ## several large applications.
388 ##
389 ## ORC is not expected to work until 2.2.
390 ## https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/23973
391
392 when resultsLent:
393 template maybeLent(T: untyped): untyped =
394 lent T
395
396 else:
397 template maybeLent(T: untyped): untyped =
398 T
399
400 func raiseResultOk[T, E](self: Result[T, E]) {.noreturn, noinline.} =
401 # noinline because raising should take as little space as possible at call
402 # site
403 when T is void:
404 raise (ref ResultError[void])(msg: "Trying to access error with value")
405 else:
406 raise (ref ResultError[T])(
407 msg: "Trying to access error with value", error: self.vResultPrivate
408 )
409
410 func raiseResultError[T, E](self: Result[T, E]) {.noreturn, noinline.} =
411 # noinline because raising should take as little space as possible at call
412 # site
413 mixin toException
414
415 when E is ref Exception:
416 if self.eResultPrivate.isNil: # for example Result.default()!
417 raise (ref ResultError[void])(msg: "Trying to access value with err (nil)")
418 raise self.eResultPrivate
419 elif E is void:
420 raise (ref ResultError[void])(msg: "Trying to access value with err")
421 elif compiles(toException(self.eResultPrivate)):
422 raise toException(self.eResultPrivate)
423 elif compiles($self.eResultPrivate):
424 raise (ref ResultError[E])(error: self.eResultPrivate, msg: $self.eResultPrivate)
425 else:
426 raise (ref ResultError[E])(
427 msg: "Trying to access value with err", error: self.eResultPrivate
428 )
429
430 ✗ func raiseResultDefect(m: string, v: auto) {.noreturn, noinline.} =
431 mixin `$`
432 when compiles($v):
433 ✗ raise (ref ResultDefect)(msg: m & ": " & $v)
434 ✗ else:
435 ✗ raise (ref ResultDefect)(msg: m)
436 ✗
437 ✗ func raiseResultDefect(m: string) {.noreturn, noinline.} =
438 ✗ raise (ref ResultDefect)(msg: m)
439 ✗
440 ✗ template withAssertOk(self: Result, body: untyped): untyped =
441 # Careful - `self` evaluated multiple times, which is fine in all current uses
442 ✗ case self.oResultPrivate
443 ✗ of false:
444 ✗ when self.E isnot void:
445 ✗ raiseResultDefect("Trying to access value with err Result", self.eResultPrivate)
446 ✗ else:
447 ✗ raiseResultDefect("Trying to access value with err Result")
448 of true:
449 body
450 7
451 template ok*[T: not void, E](R: type Result[T, E], x: untyped): R =
452 ## Initialize a result with a success and value
453 ✗ ## Example: `Result[int, string].ok(42)`
454 R(oResultPrivate: true, vResultPrivate: x)
455 7
456 7 template ok*[E](R: type Result[void, E]): R =
457 ## Initialize a result with a success and value
458 ## Example: `Result[void, string].ok()`
459 R(oResultPrivate: true)
460
461 template ok*[T: not void, E](self: var Result[T, E], x: untyped) =
462 ## Set the result to success and update value
463 ## Example: `result.ok(42)`
464 self = ok(type self, x)
465
466 template ok*[E](self: var Result[void, E]) =
467 ## Set the result to success and update value
468 ## Example: `result.ok()`
469 self = (type self).ok()
470
471 template err*[T; E: not void](R: type Result[T, E], x: untyped): R =
472 ## Initialize the result to an error
473 ## Example: `Result[int, string].err("uh-oh")`
474 R(oResultPrivate: false, eResultPrivate: x)
475
476 template err*[T](R: type Result[T, cstring], x: string): R =
477 ## Initialize the result to an error
478 ## Example: `Result[int, string].err("uh-oh")`
479 const s = x # avoid dangling cstring pointers
480 R(oResultPrivate: false, eResultPrivate: cstring(s))
481
482 template err*[T](R: type Result[T, void]): R =
483 ## Initialize the result to an error
484 ## Example: `Result[int, void].err()`
485 R(oResultPrivate: false)
486
487 template err*[T; E: not void](self: var Result[T, E], x: untyped) =
488 ## Set the result as an error
489 ## Example: `result.err("uh-oh")`
490 self = err(type self, x)
491
492 template err*[T](self: var Result[T, cstring], x: string) =
493 const s = x # Make sure we don't return a dangling pointer
494 self = err(type self, cstring(s))
495
496 template err*[T](self: var Result[T, void]) =
497 ## Set the result as an error
498 ## Example: `result.err()`
499 self = err(type self)
500
501 template ok*(v: auto): auto =
502 ok(typeof(result), v)
503
504 template ok*(): auto =
505 ok(typeof(result))
506
507 template err*(v: auto): auto =
508 err(typeof(result), v)
509
510 template err*(): auto =
511 err(typeof(result))
512
513 template isOk*(self: Result): bool =
514 self.oResultPrivate
515
516 template isErr*(self: Result): bool =
517 not self.oResultPrivate
518
519 when not defined(nimHasEffectsOfs):
520 template effectsOf(f: untyped) {.pragma, used.}
521
522 func map*[T0: not void, E; T1: not void](
523 self: Result[T0, E], f: proc(x: T0): T1
524 ): Result[T1, E] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
525 ## Transform value using f, or return error
526 ##
527 ## ```
528 ## let r = Result[int, cstring).ok(42)
529 ## assert r.map(proc (v: int): int = $v).value() == "42"
530 ## ```
531 case self.oResultPrivate
532 of true:
533 when T1 is void:
534 f(self.vResultPrivate)
535 result.ok()
536 else:
537 result.ok(f(self.vResultPrivate))
538 of false:
539 when E is void:
540 result.err()
541 else:
542 result.err(self.eResultPrivate)
543
544 func map*[T: not void, E](
545 self: Result[T, E], f: proc(x: T)
546 ): Result[void, E] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
547 ## Transform value using f, or return error
548 ##
549 ## ```
550 ## let r = Result[int, cstring).ok(42)
551 ## assert r.map(proc (v: int): int = $v).value() == "42"
552 ## ```
553 case self.oResultPrivate
554 of true:
555 f(self.vResultPrivate)
556 result.ok()
557 of false:
558 when E is void:
559 result.err()
560 else:
561 result.err(self.eResultPrivate)
562
563 func map*[E; T1: not void](
564 self: Result[void, E], f: proc(): T1
565 ): Result[T1, E] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
566 ## Transform value using f, or return error
567 case self.oResultPrivate
568 of true:
569 result.ok(f())
570 of false:
571 when E is void:
572 result.err()
573 else:
574 result.err(self.eResultPrivate)
575
576 func map*[E](
577 self: Result[void, E], f: proc()
578 ): Result[void, E] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
579 ## Call f if `self` is ok
580 case self.oResultPrivate
581 of true:
582 f()
583 result.ok()
584 of false:
585 when E is void:
586 result.err()
587 else:
588 result.err(self.eResultPrivate)
589
590 func flatMap*[T0: not void, E, T1](
591 self: Result[T0, E], f: proc(x: T0): Result[T1, E]
592 ): Result[T1, E] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
593 case self.oResultPrivate
594 of true:
595 f(self.vResultPrivate)
596 of false:
597 when E is void:
598 Result[T1, void].err()
599 else:
600 Result[T1, E].err(self.eResultPrivate)
601
602 func flatMap*[E, T1](
603 self: Result[void, E], f: proc(): Result[T1, E]
604 ): Result[T1, E] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
605 case self.oResultPrivate
606 of true:
607 f()
608 of false:
609 when E is void:
610 Result[T1, void].err()
611 else:
612 Result[T1, E].err(self.eResultPrivate)
613
614 func mapErr*[T; E0: not void, E1: not void](
615 self: Result[T, E0], f: proc(x: E0): E1
616 ): Result[T, E1] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
617 ## Transform error using f, or leave untouched
618 case self.oResultPrivate
619 of true:
620 when T is void:
621 result.ok()
622 else:
623 result.ok(self.vResultPrivate)
624 of false:
625 result.err(f(self.eResultPrivate))
626
627 func mapErr*[T; E1: not void](
628 self: Result[T, void], f: proc(): E1
629 ): Result[T, E1] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
630 ## Transform error using f, or return value
631 case self.oResultPrivate
632 of true:
633 when T is void:
634 result.ok()
635 else:
636 result.ok(self.vResultPrivate)
637 of false:
638 result.err(f())
639
640 func mapErr*[T; E0: not void](
641 self: Result[T, E0], f: proc(x: E0)
642 ): Result[T, void] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
643 ## Transform error using f, or return value
644 case self.oResultPrivate
645 of true:
646 when T is void:
647 result.ok()
648 else:
649 result.ok(self.vResultPrivate)
650 of false:
651 f(self.eResultPrivate)
652 result.err()
653
654 func mapErr*[T](
655 self: Result[T, void], f: proc()
656 ): Result[T, void] {.inline, effectsOf: f.} =
657 ## Transform error using f, or return value
658 case self.oResultPrivate
659 of true:
660 when T is void:
661 result.ok()
662 else:
663 result.ok(self.vResultPrivate)
664 of false:
665 f()
666 result.err()
667
668 func mapConvert*[T0: not void, E](
669 self: Result[T0, E], T1: type
670 ): Result[T1, E] {.inline.} =
671 ## Convert result value to A using an conversion
672 # Would be nice if it was automatic...
673 case self.oResultPrivate
674 of true:
675 when T1 is void:
676 result.ok()
677 else:
678 result.ok(T1(self.vResultPrivate))
679 of false:
680 when E is void:
681 result.err()
682 else:
683 result.err(self.eResultPrivate)
684
685 func mapCast*[T0: not void, E](
686 self: Result[T0, E], T1: type
687 ): Result[T1, E] {.inline.} =
688 ## Convert result value to A using a cast
689 ## Would be nice with nicer syntax...
690 case self.oResultPrivate
691 of true:
692 when T1 is void:
693 result.ok()
694 else:
695 result.ok(cast[T1](self.vResultPrivate))
696 of false:
697 when E is void:
698 result.err()
699 else:
700 result.err(self.eResultPrivate)
701
702 func mapConvertErr*[T, E0](self: Result[T, E0], E1: type): Result[T, E1] {.inline.} =
703 ## Convert result error to E1 using an conversion
704 # Would be nice if it was automatic...
705 when E0 is E1:
706 result = self
707 else:
708 if self.oResultPrivate:
709 when T is void:
710 result.ok()
711 else:
712 result.ok(self.vResultPrivate)
713 else:
714 when E1 is void:
715 result.err()
716 else:
717 result.err(E1(self.eResultPrivate))
718
719 func mapCastErr*[T, E0](self: Result[T, E0], E1: type): Result[T, E1] {.inline.} =
720 ## Convert result value to A using a cast
721 ## Would be nice with nicer syntax...
722 if self.oResultPrivate:
723 when T is void:
724 result.ok()
725 else:
726 result.ok(self.vResultPrivate)
727 else:
728 result.err(cast[E1](self.eResultPrivate))
729
730 template `and`*[T0, E, T1](self: Result[T0, E], other: Result[T1, E]): Result[T1, E] =
731 ## Evaluate `other` iff self.isOk, else return error
732 ## fail-fast - will not evaluate other if a is an error
733 let s = (self) # TODO avoid copy
734 case s.oResultPrivate
735 of true:
736 other
737 of false:
738 when type(self) is type(other):
739 s
740 else:
741 type R = type(other)
742 when E is void:
743 err(R)
744 else:
745 err(R, s.eResultPrivate)
746
747 template `or`*[T, E0, E1](self: Result[T, E0], other: Result[T, E1]): Result[T, E1] =
748 ## Evaluate `other` iff `not self.isOk`, else return `self`
749 ## fail-fast - will not evaluate `other` if `self` is ok
750 ##
751 ## ```
752 ## func f(): Result[int, SomeEnum] =
753 ## f2() or err(SomeEnum.V) # Collapse errors from other module / function
754 ## ```
755 let s = (self) # TODO avoid copy
756 case s.oResultPrivate
757 of true:
758 when type(self) is type(other):
759 s
760 else:
761 type R = type(other)
762 when T is void:
763 ok(R)
764 else:
765 ok(R, s.vResultPrivate)
766 of false:
767 other
768
769 template orErr*[T, E0, E1](self: Result[T, E0], error: E1): Result[T, E1] =
770 ## Evaluate `other` iff `not self.isOk`, else return `self`
771 ## fail-fast - will not evaluate `error` if `self` is ok
772 ##
773 ## ```
774 ## func f(): Result[int, SomeEnum] =
775 ## f2().orErr(SomeEnum.V) # Collapse errors from other module / function
776 ## ```
777 ##
778 ## ** Experimental, may be removed **
779 let s = (self) # TODO avoid copy
780 type R = Result[T, E1]
781 case s.oResultPrivate
782 of true:
783 when type(self) is R:
784 s
785 else:
786 when T is void:
787 ok(R)
788 else:
789 ok(R, s.vResultPrivate)
790 of false:
791 err(R, error)
792
793 template catch*(body: typed): Result[type(body), ref CatchableError] =
794 ## Catch exceptions for body and store them in the Result
795 ##
796 ## ```
797 ## let r = catch: someFuncThatMayRaise()
798 ## ```
799 type R = Result[type(body), ref CatchableError]
800
801 try:
802 when type(body) is void:
803 body
804

910 assert self.oResultPrivate # Emulate field access defect in debug builds
911
912 func tryValue*[E](self: Result[void, E]) {.inline.} =
913 ## Fetch value of result if set, or raise
914 ## When E is an Exception, raise that exception - otherwise, raise a ResultError[E]
915 mixin raiseResultError
916 case self.oResultPrivate
917 of false:
918 self.raiseResultError()
919 of true:
920 discard
921
922 func tryValue*[T: not void, E](self: Result[T, E]): maybeLent T {.inline.} =
923 ## Fetch value of result if set, or raise
924 ## When E is an Exception, raise that exception - otherwise, raise a ResultError[E]
925 mixin raiseResultError
926 case self.oResultPrivate
927 of false:
928 self.raiseResultError()
929 of true:
930 # TODO https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/22216
931 result = self.vResultPrivate
932
933 func expect*[E](self: Result[void, E], m: string) =
934 ## Return value of Result, or raise a `Defect` with the given message - use
935 ## this helper to extract the value when an error is not expected, for example
936 ## because the program logic dictates that the operation should never fail
937 ##
938 ## ```nim
939 ## let r = Result[int, int].ok(42)
940 ## # Put here a helpful comment why you think this won't fail
941 ## echo r.expect("r was just set to ok(42)")
942 ## ```
943 case self.oResultPrivate
944 of false:
945 when E isnot void:
946 raiseResultDefect(m, self.eResultPrivate)
947 else:
948 raiseResultDefect(m)
949 of true:
950 discard
951
952 func expect*[T: not void, E](self: Result[T, E], m: string): maybeLent T =
953 ## Return value of Result, or raise a `Defect` with the given message - use
954 ## this helper to extract the value when an error is not expected, for example
955 ## because the program logic dictates that the operation should never fail
956 ##
957 ## ```nim
958 ## let r = Result[int, int].ok(42)
959 ## # Put here a helpful comment why you think this won't fail
960 ## echo r.expect("r was just set to ok(42)")
961 ## ```
962 case self.oResultPrivate
963 of false:
964 when E isnot void:
965 raiseResultDefect(m, self.eResultPrivate)
966 else:
967 raiseResultDefect(m)
968 of true:
969 # TODO https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/22216
970 result = self.vResultPrivate
971
972 func expect*[T: not void, E](self: var Result[T, E], m: string): var T =
973 (
974 case self.oResultPrivate
975 of false:
976 when E isnot void:
977 raiseResultDefect(m, self.eResultPrivate)
978 else:
979 raiseResultDefect(m)
980 of true:
981 addr self.vResultPrivate
982 )[]
983
984 ✗ func `$`*[T, E](self: Result[T, E]): string =
985 ## Returns string representation of `self`
986 ✗ case self.oResultPrivate
987 of true:
988 when T is void:
989 ✗ "ok()"
990 ✗ else:
991 ✗ "ok(" & $self.vResultPrivate & ")"
992 ✗ of false:
993 when E is void:
994 "none()"
995 else:
996 ✗ "err(" & $self.eResultPrivate & ")"
997 ✗
998 func error*[T](self: Result[T, void]) =
999 ## Fetch error of result if set, or raise Defect
1000 ✗ case self.oResultPrivate
1001 of true:
1002 ✗ when T isnot void:
1003 ✗ raiseResultDefect("Trying to access error when value is set", self.vResultPrivate)
1004 else:
1005 raiseResultDefect("Trying to access error when value is set")
1006 of false:
1007 discard

 31 result = self.eResultPrivate
1020 31
1021 func tryError*[T](self: Result[T, void]) {.inline.} =
1022 ## Fetch error of result if set, or raise
1023 ✗ ## Raises a ResultError[T]
1024 mixin raiseResultOk
1025 31 case self.oResultPrivate
1026 31 of true:
1027 self.raiseResultOk()
1028 of false:
1029 discard
1030
1031 func tryError*[T; E: not void](self: Result[T, E]): maybeLent E {.inline.} =
1032 ## Fetch error of result if set, or raise
1033 ## Raises a ResultError[T]
1034 mixin raiseResultOk
1035 case self.oResultPrivate
1036 of true:
1037 self.raiseResultOk()
1038 of false:
1039 # TODO https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/22216
1040 result = self.eResultPrivate
1041
1042 template unsafeError*[T; E: not void](self: Result[T, E]): E =
1043 ## Fetch error of result if set, undefined behavior if unset
1044 ## See also: `unsafeValue`
1045 self.eResultPrivate
1046
1047 template unsafeError*[T](self: Result[T, void]) =
1048 ## Fetch error of result if set, undefined behavior if unset
1049 ## See also: `unsafeValue`
1050 assert not self.oResultPrivate # Emulate field access defect in debug builds
1051
1052 func optValue*[T, E](self: Result[T, E]): Opt[T] =
1053 ## Return the value of a Result as an Opt, or none if Result is an error
1054 case self.oResultPrivate
1055 of true:
1056 when T is void:
1057 Opt[void].ok()
1058 else:
1059 Opt.some(self.vResultPrivate)
1060 of false:
1061 Opt.none(T)
1062
1063 func optError*[T, E](self: Result[T, E]): Opt[E] =
1064 ## Return the error of a Result as an Opt, or none if Result is a value
1065 case self.oResultPrivate
1066 of true:
1067 Opt.none(E)
1068 of false:
1069 Opt.some(self.eResultPrivate)
1070
1071 # Alternative spellings for `value`, for `options` compatibility
1072 template get*[T: not void, E](self: Result[T, E]): T =
1073 self.value()
1074
1075 template get*[E](self: Result[void, E]) =
1076 self.value()
1077
1078 template tryGet*[T: not void, E](self: Result[T, E]): T =
1079 self.tryValue()
1080
1081 template tryGet*[E](self: Result[void, E]) =
1082 self.tryValue()
1083
1084 template unsafeGet*[T: not void, E](self: Result[T, E]): T =
1085 self.unsafeValue()
1086
1087 template unsafeGet*[E](self: Result[void, E]) =
1088 self.unsafeValue()
1089
1090 # `var` overloads should not be needed but result in invalid codegen (!):
1091 # https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/22049 (fixed in 1.6.16+)
1092 1 func get*[T: not void, E](self: var Result[T, E]): var T =
1093

1094 1
1095 func get*[T, E](self: Result[T, E], otherwise: T): T {.inline.} =
1096 ## Fetch value of result if set, or return the value `otherwise`
1097 ## See `valueOr` for a template version that avoids evaluating `otherwise`
1098 ## unless necessary
1099 case self.oResultPrivate
1100 of true: self.vResultPrivate
1101 of false: otherwise
1102
1103 when resultsGenericsOpenSymWorkaround:
1104 import macros
1105
1106 proc containsHack(n: NimNode): bool =
1107 if n.len == 0:
1108 n.eqIdent("isOkOr") or n.eqIdent("isErrOr") or n.eqIdent("valueOr") or
1109 n.eqIdent("errorOr")
1110 else:
1111 for child in n:
1112 if containsHack(child):
1113 return true
1114 false
1115
1116 proc containsIdent(n: NimNode, what: string, with: NimNode): bool =
1117 if n == with:
1118 false # Don't replace if the right symbol is already being used
1119 elif n.eqIdent(what):
1120 true
1121 else:
1122 for child in n:
1123 if containsIdent(child, what, with):
1124 return true
1125
1126 false
1127
1128 proc replace(n: NimNode, what: string, with: NimNode): NimNode =
1129 if not containsIdent(n, what, with): # Fast path that avoids copies altogether
1130 return n
1131
1132 if n == with:
1133 result = with
1134 elif n.eqIdent(what):
1135 when resultsGenericsOpenSymWorkaroundHint:
1136 hint("Replaced conflicting external symbol " & what, n)
1137 result = with
1138 else:
1139 case n.kind
1140 of nnkCallKinds:
1141 if n[0].containsHack():
1142 # Don't replace inside nested expansion
1143 result = n
1144 elif n[0].eqIdent(what):
1145 if n.len == 1:
1146 if n[0] == with:
1147 result = n
1148 else:
1149 # No arguments - replace call symbol
1150 result = copyNimNode(n)
1151 result.add with
1152 when resultsGenericsOpenSymWorkaroundHint:
1153 hint("Replaced conflicting external symbol " & what, n[0])
1154 else:
1155 # `error(...)` - replace args but not function name
1156 result = copyNimNode(n)
1157 result.add n[0]
1158 for i in 1 ..< n.len:
1159 result.add replace(n[i], what, with)
1160 else:
1161 result = copyNimNode(n)
1162 for i in 0 ..< n.len:
1163 result.add replace(n[i], what, with)
1164 of nnkExprEqExpr:
1165 # "error = xxx" - function call with named parameters and other weird stuff
1166 result = copyNimNode(n)
1167 result.add n[0]
1168 for i in 1 ..< n.len:
1169 result.add replace(n[i], what, with)
1170 of nnkLetSection, nnkVarSection, nnkFormalParams:
1171 result = copyNimNode(n)
1172 for i in 0 ..< n.len:
1173 result.add replace(n[i], what, with)
1174 of nnkDotExpr:
1175 # Ignore rhs in "abc.error"
1176 result = copyNimNode(n)
1177 result.add(replace(n[0], what, with))
1178 result.add(n[1])
1179 else:
1180 if (
1181 n.kind == nnkForStmt and
1182 (n[0].eqIdent(what) or (n.len == 4 and n[1].eqIdent(what))) or
1183 (n.kind == nnkIdentDefs and n[0].eqIdent(what))
1184 ):
1185 # Naming the symbol the same way requires lots of magic here - just
1186 # say no
1187 error("Shadowing variable declarations of `" & what & "` not supported", n[0])
1188
1189 result = copyNimNode(n)
1190 for i in 0 ..< n.len:
1191 result.add replace(n[i], what, with)
1192
1193 macro replaceHack(body, what, with: untyped): untyped =
1194 # This hack replaces the `what` identifier with `with` except where
1195 # this replacing is not expected - this is an approximation of the intent
1196 # of injecting a template and likely doesn't cover all applicable cases
1197 result = replace(body, $what, with)
1198
1199 template isOkOr*[T, E](self: Result[T, E], body: untyped) =
1200 ## Evaluate `body` iff result has been assigned an error
1201 ## `body` is evaluated lazily.
1202 ##
1203 ## Example:
1204 ## ```
1205 ## let
1206 ## v = Result[int, string].err("hello")
1207 ## x = v.isOkOr: echo "not ok"
1208 ## # experimental: direct error access using an unqualified `error` symbol
1209 ## z = v.isOkOr: echo error
1210 ## ```
1211 ##
1212 ## `error` access:
1213 ##
1214 ## TODO experimental, might change in the future
1215 ##
1216 ## The template contains a shortcut for accessing the error of the result,
1217 ## it can only be used outside of generic code,
1218 ## see https://github.com/status-im/nim-stew/issues/161#issuecomment-1397121386
1219
1220 let s = (self) # TODO avoid copy
1221 case s.oResultPrivate
1222 of false:
1223 when E isnot void:
1224 template error(): E {.used, gensym.} =
1225 s.eResultPrivate
1226
1227 replaceHack(body, "error", error)
1228 else:
1229 body
1230 of true:
1231 discard
1232
1233 template isErrOr*[T, E](self: Result[T, E], body: untyped) =
1234 ## Evaluate `body` iff result has been assigned a value
1235 ## `body` is evaluated lazily.
1236 ##
1237 ## Example:
1238 ## ```
1239 ## let
1240 ## v = Result[int, string].ok(42)
1241 ## x = v.isErrOr: echo "not err"
1242 ## # experimental: direct value access using an unqualified `value` symbol
1243 ## z = v.isErrOr: echo value
1244 ## ```
1245 ##
1246 ## `value` access:
1247 ##
1248 ## TODO experimental, might change in the future
1249 ##
1250 ## The template contains a shortcut for accessing the value of the result,
1251 ## it can only be used outside of generic code,
1252 ## see https://github.com/status-im/nim-stew/issues/161#issuecomment-1397121386
1253
1254 let s = (self) # TODO avoid copy
1255 case s.oResultPrivate
1256 of true:
1257 when T isnot void:
1258 template value(): T {.used, gensym.} =
1259 s.vResultPrivate
1260
1261 replaceHack(body, "value", s.vResultPrivate)
1262 else:
1263 body
1264 of false:
1265 discard
1266
1267 template valueOr*[T: not void, E](self: Result[T, E], def: untyped): T =
1268 ## Fetch value of result if set, or evaluate `def`
1269 ## `def` is evaluated lazily, and must be an expression of `T` or exit
1270 ## the scope (for example using `return` / `raise`)
1271 ##
1272 ## See `isOkOr` for a version that works with `Result[void, E]`.
1273 ##
1274 ## Example:
1275 ## ```
1276 ## let
1277 ## v = Result[int, string].err("hello")
1278 ## x = v.valueOr: 42 # x == 42 now
1279 ## y = v.valueOr: raise (ref ValueError)(msg: "v is an error, gasp!")
1280 ## # experimental: direct error access using an unqualified `error` symbol
1281 ## z = v.valueOr: raise (ref ValueError)(msg: error)
1282 ## ```
1283 ##
1284 ## `error` access:
1285 ##
1286 ## TODO experimental, might change in the future
1287 ##
1288 ## The template contains a shortcut for accessing the error of the result,
1289 ## it can only be used outside of generic code,
1290 ## see https://github.com/status-im/nim-stew/issues/161#issuecomment-1397121386
1291 ##
1292 let s = (self) # TODO avoid copy
1293 case s.oResultPrivate
1294 of true:
1295 s.vResultPrivate
1296 of false:
1297 when E isnot void:
1298 template error(): E {.used, gensym.} =
1299 s.eResultPrivate
1300
1301 replaceHack(def, "error", error)
1302 else:
1303 def
1304
1305 template errorOr*[T; E: not void](self: Result[T, E], def: untyped): E =
1306 ## Fetch error of result if not set, or evaluate `def`
1307 ## `def` is evaluated lazily, and must be an expression of `T` or exit
1308 ## the scope (for example using `return` / `raise`)
1309 ##
1310 ## See `isErrOr` for a version that works with `Result[T, void]`.
1311 let s = (self) # TODO avoid copy
1312 case s.oResultPrivate
1313 of false:
1314 s.eResultPrivate
1315 of true:
1316 when T isnot void:
1317 template value(): T {.used, gensym.} =
1318 s.vResultPrivate
1319
1320 replaceHack(def, "value", value)
1321 else:
1322 def
1323
1324 else:
1325 # TODO https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/pull/23892#discussion_r1713434311
1326 const pushGenericsOpenSym = defined(nimHasGenericsOpenSym2) and resultsGenericsOpenSym
1327
1328 template isOkOr*[T, E](self: Result[T, E], body: untyped) =
1329 ## Evaluate `body` iff result has been assigned an error
1330 ## `body` is evaluated lazily.
1331 ##
1332 ## Example:
1333 ## ```
1334 ## let
1335 ## v = Result[int, string].err("hello")
1336 ## x = v.isOkOr: echo "not ok"
1337 ## # experimental: direct error access using an unqualified `error` symbol
1338 ## z = v.isOkOr: echo error
1339 ## ```
1340 ##
1341 ## `error` access:
1342 ##
1343 ## TODO experimental, might change in the future
1344 ##
1345 ## The template contains a shortcut for accessing the error of the result,
1346 ## it can only be used outside of generic code,
1347 ## see https://github.com/status-im/nim-stew/issues/161#issuecomment-1397121386
1348
1349 let s = (self) # TODO avoid copy
1350 case s.oResultPrivate
1351 of false:
1352 when E isnot void:
1353 when pushGenericsOpenSym:
1354 {.push experimental: "genericsOpenSym".}
1355 template error(): E {.used.} =
1356 s.eResultPrivate
1357
1358 body
1359 of true:
1360 discard
1361
1362 template isErrOr*[T, E](self: Result[T, E], body: untyped) =

1380 ## it can only be used outside of generic code,
1381 ## see https://github.com/status-im/nim-stew/issues/161#issuecomment-1397121386

1453 func flatten*[T, E](self: Result[Result[T, E], E]): Result[T, E] =
1454 ## Remove one level of nesting
1455 case self.oResultPrivate
1456 of true:
1457 self.vResultPrivate
1458 of false:
1459 when E is void:
1460 err(Result[T, E])
1461 else:
1462 err(Result[T, E], self.error)
1463
1464 func filter*[T, E](
1465 self: Result[T, E], callback: proc(x: T): Result[void, E]
1466 ): Result[T, E] {.effectsOf: callback.} =
1467 ## Apply `callback` to the `self`, iff `self` is not an error. If `callback`
1468 ## returns an error, return that error, else return `self`
1469
1470 case self.oResultPrivate
1471 of true:
1472 callback(self.vResultPrivate) and self
1473 of false:
1474 self
1475
1476 func filter*[E](
1477 self: Result[void, E], callback: proc(): Result[void, E]
1478 ): Result[void, E] {.effectsOf: callback.} =
1479 ## Apply `callback` to the `self`, iff `self` is not an error. If `callback`
1480 ## returns an error, return that error, else return `self`
1481
1482 case self.oResultPrivate
1483 of true:
1484 callback() and self
1485 of false:
1486 self
1487
1488 func filter*[T](
1489 self: Result[T, void], callback: proc(x: T): bool
1490 ): Result[T, void] {.effectsOf: callback.} =
1491 ## Apply `callback` to the `self`, iff `self` is not an error. If `callback`
1492 ## returns an error, return that error, else return `self`
1493
1494 case self.oResultPrivate
1495 of true:
1496 if callback(self.vResultPrivate):
1497 self
1498 else:
1499 Result[T, void].err()
1500 of false:
1501 self
1502
1503 # Options compatibility
1504
1505 template some*[T](O: type Opt, v: T): Opt[T] =
1506 ## Create an `Opt` set to a value
1507 ##
1508 ## ```
1509 ## let oResultPrivate = Opt.some(42)
1510 ## assert oResultPrivate.isSome and oResultPrivate.value() == 42
1511 ## ```
1512 Opt[T].ok(v)
1513
1514 template none*(O: type Opt, T: type): Opt[T] =
1515 ## Create an `Opt` set to none
1516 ##
1517 ## ```
1518 ## let oResultPrivate = Opt.none(int)
1519 ## assert oResultPrivate.isNone
1520 ## ```
1521 ✗ Opt[T].err()
1522
1523 template isSome*(oResultPrivate: Opt): bool =
1524 ## Alias for `isOk`
1525 isOk oResultPrivate
1526
1527 template isNone*(oResultPrivate: Opt): bool =
1528 ## Alias of `isErr`
1529 isErr oResultPrivate
1530
1531 # Syntactic convenience
1532
1533 template `?`*[T, E](self: Result[T, E]): auto =
1534 ## Early return - if self is an error, we will return from the current
1535 ## function, else we'll move on..
1536 ##
1537 ## ```
1538 ## let v = ? funcWithResult()
1539 ## echo v # prints value, not Result!
1540 ## ```
1541 ## Experimental
1542 # TODO the v copy is here to prevent multiple evaluations of self - could
1543 # probably avoid it with some fancy macro magic..
1544

✓ Branch 3 taken 74 times.
 727 let v = (self)
1545 case v.oResultPrivate
1546 of false:
1547 # Instead of `return xxx` we use `result = xxx; return` which avoids the
1548 # need to rewrite `return` in macros that take over the `result` symbol and
1549 # its associated control flow - this hack increases compatibility with
1550 # chronos' async - see https://github.com/status-im/nim-stew/issues/37 for
1551 # more in-depth discussion.
1552 when typeof(result) is typeof(v):
1553 ✗ result = v
1554 ✗ return

1556 when E is void:
1557 result = err(typeof(result))
1558 return

1560 result = err(typeof(result), v.eResultPrivate)
1561 return

1563 when not (T is void):
1564 v.vResultPrivate

1566 # Collection integration
1567
1568 iterator values*[T, E](self: Result[T, E]): maybeLent T =

1570 case self.oResultPrivate
1571 of true:
1572 yield self.vResultPrivate
1573 of false:
1574 discard
1575

1580 yield self.eResultPrivate
1581 of true:
1582 discard

1587 of true:

1589 of false:

1613 func containsValue*(self: Result, v: auto): bool =
1614 ## Return true iff the given result is set to a value that equals `v`
1615 case self.oResultPrivate
1616 of true:
1617 self.vResultPrivate == v
1618 of false:
1619 false
1620
1621 func containsError*(self: Result, e: auto): bool =
1622 ## Return true iff the given result is set to an error that equals `e`
1623 case self.oResultPrivate
1624 of false:
1625 self.eResultPrivate == e
1626 of true:
1627 false
1628
1629 func contains*(self: Opt, v: auto): bool =
1630 ## Return true iff the given `Opt` is set to a value that equals `v` - can
1631 ## also be used in the "infix" `in` form:
1632 ##
1633 ## ```nim
1634 ## assert "value" in Opt.some("value")
1635 ## ```
1636 case self.oResultPrivate
1637 of true:
1638 self.vResultPrivate == v
1639 of false:
1640 false
